untranslatable_reason: Signal requires identification of 'significant directional leg', 'pullback retracement percentage thresholds', and 'relative volume comparison to prior leg' — these multi-bar swing analysis concepts cannot be expressed as single-bar indicator conditions. MACD used as proxy for momentum indicator, but the core logic of comparing pullback strength relative to prior move magnitude requires custom bar-pattern logic not available in standard indicators.
translation_notes: This signal fundamentally describes swing-level price action analysis (identifying legs, measuring retracements, comparing volumes across multi-bar moves) rather than single-bar indicator states. The entry requires: (1) confirming prior directional move via momentum extremes, (2) detecting pullback retracement depth, (3) assessing pullback volume vs prior leg volume, and (4) confirming momentum has not reversed. Without swing-tracking logic or lookback arrays, only MACD momentum direction can be approximated. A proper backtest engine implementation would need custom logic to identify swing highs/lows, measure leg magnitudes, calculate retracement percentages, and compare bar-group statistics.
